rationale: Direct alignment. Regulatory compliance control should manage state framework requirements and local policy implementation.
Strong alignment. Privacy notice and data handling controls support student-data safeguards.
Direct alignment. Acceptable-use controls are the best fit for permitted/prohibited school AI uses.
Strong alignment. Safety/security controls cover secure use and misuse prevention.
Strong alignment. Training controls support literacy and educator preparedness.
Direct alignment. Vendor risk management is the appropriate ARISE control for school procurement and vendor data/privacy assurances.
Direct alignment. The law is principally about safe chatbot operation and prevention of harmful interactions.
Strong alignment. User notice controls support transparency obligations for AI interactions.
Partial alignment. AI-specific attack-vector controls support misuse prevention but do not fully cover child-safety legal duties.
Strong alignment. Testing validates chatbot disclosures and safety constraints.
